summary: Matched several previously stubbed menu/game-mode functions, mainly introducing the first substantive mninfo.c/mninfo.h implementation plus two gm_16F1.c matches. The PR added MnInfoData layout knowledge, mnInfo achievement-counting/filtering helpers, text-column setup, scroll-arrow visibility logic, and small game-mode unlock/flag-check routines. Header prototypes were tightened where signatures became known, including bool fn_80164B48(void) and bool gm_801721EC(void).
what_changed: Added mninfo.c implementation content where the file had effectively been empty, including includes for baselib GObj/JObj/SisLib, gmmain_lib, lb_00F9, lblanguage, mnmain, and un_2FC9.

Implemented mnInfo_80251A08(s32), a wrapper around gmMainLib_8015D94C with special cases for IDs 0x3E, 0x34, and 0x35; the 0x34/0x35 cases are filtered by lbLang_IsSettingUS() and lbLang_IsSettingJP().
Implemented mnInfo_80251AA4(void), counting enabled entries over range 0..0x41 using mnInfo_80251A08; the function was protected with #pragma dont_inline.

Implemented mnInfo_80251F04(HSD_GObj* gobj, u32 idx, u32 arg2), replacing an existing right-column text object if present, creating a new HSD_Text via HSD_SisLib_803A5ACC, configuring scale/flags, resolving a string ID through un_802FE3F8, and appending text through HSD_SisLib_803A6368.

Added static Vec3 mnInfo_803EFC08[0x12] data used by mnInfo_802522B8; many entries appear as raw float reinterpretations rather than semantically named coordinates, so the exact meaning remains weakly evidenced.

Implemented mnInfo_802522B8(HSD_GObj*), which toggles JOBJ_HIDDEN on two child JOBjs based on scroll_idx and whether more than four achievement/info entries exist, then calls mn_8022ED6C(jobj, mnInfo_803EFC08).
Implemented fn_802523B8(HSD_GObj*) as a thin wrapper around HSD_GObjPLink_80390228.
Implemented mnInfo_80252720(MnInfoData*) to initialize scroll_idx, anim_timer, description, and four left/right column pointers.
Added mninfo.h guard, MnInfoData struct, an M2CTX-only MenuInfo_GObj struct override for typed user_data access, and prototypes for matched/still-unmatched mnInfo functions.

Implemented gm_801721EC(void) in gm_16F1.c using four static inline helpers: two scan gmMainLib_8015D94C/gmMainLib_8015D8D8 over 0x42 entries and two scan gmMainLib_8015DA1C over 0x125 entries, returning true if either scan finds a match.
Implemented fn_8017280C(void), dispatching paired gmMainLib_8015EE* calls based on gmMainLib_8015D94C for IDs 0x18, 0x17, 0x16, and 0x1B.
Corrected gm_16F1.h prototype for gm_801721EC from bool gm_801721EC(UNK_PARAMS) to bool gm_801721EC(void).
Corrected gm_1601.h prototype for fn_80164B48 from int fn_80164B48(void) to bool fn_80164B48(void).
smart_moves: Used small static inline helper functions around gm_801721EC to shape the generated code for a match while keeping the top-level boolean logic readable.
Preserved known-but-not-yet-matched function gaps with /// #function stubs rather than overclaiming completion.
Used #pragma dont_inline on mnInfo_80251AA4 and mnInfo_802522B8, indicating inlining behavior mattered for matching these functions.
Introduced a dedicated MnInfoData struct instead of treating the menu user_data as a generic menu blob, making scroll state, text columns, and description ownership explicit.
Added an M2CTX-only typed MenuInfo_GObj overlay so m2c/context generation can see MnInfoData user_data while normal builds continue using HSD_GObj.
Tightened header signatures once evidence showed no parameters and boolean returns, reducing future UNK_PARAMS churn.
decomp_lessons: Menu files often need local menu-specific user_data structs; generic menu types may be too coarse for matching and understanding ownership.
Language-region checks can be part of menu availability/filtering logic; here 0x34 and 0x35 are conditionally suppressed based on US/JP language settings before falling back to gmMainLib_8015D94C.
For simple boolean scans, matching may require explicit helper functions and straightforward true/false returns instead of collapsing into compact expressions.
Scroll-arrow visibility in menu code can be matched by retrieving child JOBjs with lb_80011E24 and toggling JOBJ_HIDDEN via HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll/HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll.
When initializing small fixed-size menu arrays, explicit element assignments may match better than loops or memset-style initialization.
Raw data arrays recovered as floats may represent embedded data that is not yet semantically understood; keep them local/static and avoid overnaming until more evidence exists.
Header cleanup should accompany function matches: replacing UNK_PARAMS and broad integer returns with exact void/bool signatures prevents later mismatch propagation.
naming_conventions: New menu symbols follow the existing address-based mnInfo_8025xxxx naming convention until semantic names are known.
Local match-shaping helpers for gm_801721EC were named with suffixes gm_801721EC_1 through gm_801721EC_4, reflecting their relationship to the parent function rather than inferred semantics.
The data struct was named MnInfoData and fields were named for observed role: scroll_idx, anim_timer, left_column, right_column, and description.
The M2CTX-only typed GObj overlay was named MenuInfo_GObj to distinguish it from the normal HSD_GObj typedef path.
Still-unidentified functions retain fn_ or address-based names and UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes where not matched.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: #pragma dont_inline was used around mnInfo_80251AA4 and mnInfo_802522B8 to preserve expected call/inlining structure.
Static inline wrappers were used in gm_801721EC to produce the desired control flow while scanning fixed ranges 0x42 and 0x125.
PAD_STACK(12) was inserted in mnInfo_802522B8, implying stack layout padding was needed for a match.
Explicit true/false branches were retained instead of returning compound boolean expressions, likely matching the original codegen pattern.
Explicit initialization of each MnInfoData text pointer was used in mnInfo_80252720 instead of compact aggregate or loop initialization.
A local s16 stack variable was passed to un_802FE3F8 in mnInfo_80251F04 before casting through u16/s32 for HSD_SisLib_803A6368, preserving the observed stack/temp behavior.
Function signatures were narrowed in headers only when the implementation established exact return/parameter shapes.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

The mninfo.h comments include author-side uncertainty: each menu probably has its own struct rather than the generic Menu type, and the M2CTX MenuInfo_GObj overlay was attempted to help fixes but was described as unsuccessful. This is evidence of exploratory struct/context work, not reviewer feedback.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching mninfo.c or mninfo.h to see whether MnInfoData fields or mnInfo_803EFC08 received better semantic names.
Search for future changes to gm_801721EC helper structure to determine whether the inline helper tactic was kept or simplified.
Search for uses of MenuInfo_GObj and M2CTX overlays in other menu files to compare struct/context-generation patterns.
Investigate whether the raw-looking mnInfo_803EFC08 Vec3 constants were later split into asset data or retyped.
Look for later matches of mnInfo_80251AFC, mnInfo_80251D58, fn_80251FE4, fn_802523D8, fn_80252548, and mnInfo_80252758.
